title: ophiorrhiza rugosa var. prostrata
intro: Ophiorrhiza rugosa var. prostrata, also known as the creeping snakebine, is a variant of the Ophiorrhiza rugosa plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in India and China, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic activities. The health benefits of Ophiorrhiza rugosa var. prostrata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Ophiorrhiza rugosa var. prostrata include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
